title: How Affirm Withdrew from the Australian Market
rationale: Exited Australia <18 months after entry (wind-down from Feb 2023); first country Affirm ever closed.

title: How ANNA Money Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Established ANZ beachhead in 2024 via acquisition of Sydney's GetCape (its first international expansion); operating, not yet visibly scaling.

title: How Banked Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Entered via NAB partnership/investment; Pay by Bank commercially live with NAB merchants H1 2024 — established foothold.

title: How Binance Australia Derivatives Lost Its AFSL
rationale: ASIC cancelled its AFSL (Apr 2023) plus A$10M penalty and A$13.1M client compensation; the licensed derivatives entry ended.

title: How Blue Prism Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Successful ANZ entry (Telstra anchor); company's corporate outcome was acquisition by SS&C for GBP1.27bn (2022).

title: How Carl's Jr. Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Master franchisee CJ's Group entered voluntary administration Jul 2024, liquidated Nov 2024; 20 stores closed.

title: How Catch.com.au Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Wound down Jan-Jun 2025 after ~A$450M cumulative losses under Wesfarmers; ~200 jobs lost.

title: How Clanwilliam Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Built a dominant, embedded ANZ clinical-communications platform via acquisitions (HealthLink, Telstra Health assets); established position.

title: How ComfortDelGro Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Explicit multi-modal scaling strategy; successive acquisitions culminating in A2B to build Australia's largest combined taxi network.

title: How ComplyAdvantage Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Established APAC presence (Sydney MD, FinTech Australia member) ahead of the Tranche 2 regulatory wave; positioned but early.

title: How Contino Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Entered Jan 2017, acquired Nebulr 2018, sold to Cognizant 2019 (~2 years); ANZ client base framed as the exit catalyst.

title: How Daon Entered the ANZ Market: Winning Government Trust with Biometrics — From Border Crossings to Banking
rationale: Established, trusted ANZ position across NZ government (passports, RealMe, MSD) extending into banking; durable, not high-growth narrative.

title: How Darktrace Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Strong ANZ growth (60%+ YoY); globally IPO'd 2021 then taken private by Thoma Bravo 2024 — end-state acquired, though IPO also a candidate.

title: How DaXtra Technologies Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: 15+ years of continuous, durable ANZ presence anchored by Peoplebank; explicitly a patient platform play, not a rocket ship.

title: How Debenhams Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Opened only one Melbourne store then quietly closed it; no physical retail operation remains.

title: How Deliveroo Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: ANZ operation exited Nov 2022 into voluntary administration despite peak scale; framed as an exit to study.

title: How Dext Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Became the default pre-accounting tool via deep Xero integration; established ANZ distribution and 1M+ global users.

title: How Esprit Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Closed all 67 ANZ stores by end-2018 with 350 job losses after years of losses; redirected to Asia.

title: How Featurespace Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Successful eftpos-anchored ANZ entry; corporate outcome was acquisition by HSBC (2023).

title: How Fenergo Entered the ANZ Market: Regulatory Urgency as a Wedge — How Fenergo Won Four of Australia's Biggest Banks
rationale: Established category position — selected by four of Australia's top-five banks by 2017 off a 2014 Sydney entry.

title: How Fexco Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: 15-year NZ Pacific base (400+ staff) now actively expanding into Australian retail (2024, 20+ outlet / 80+ job plan).

title: How FINEOS Entered the ANZ Market: Category Dominance Through Vertical Focus — How FINEOS Became the Standard for ANZ Insurance
rationale: Category-dominant, established: >70% of Australian life premiums on FINEOS Claims, anchor ACC NZ contract.

title: How Foodora Exited the Australian Market
rationale: Announced Australian exit and entered voluntary administration in 2018 amid Fair Work sham-contracting proceedings.

title: How Gap Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Franchise discontinued; all six Australian stores closed by Jan 2018, leaving no physical presence.

title: How Groupon Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Never achieved a viable position; the Australian group-buying operation became marginal and declined.

title: How Kaufland Cancelled Its Australian Launch
rationale: Cancelled the launch entirely in Jan 2020 after ~A$523M spent, without opening a single store.

title: How Kyckr Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Used an ASX listing itself as the market-entry mechanism (public listing as trust signal); revenue grew post-listing.

title: How Laybuy Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Entered receivership Jun 2024; ASX-delisted 2023 and assets sold to Klarna — failed entry.

title: How LearnUpon Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Actively growing: 100+ APAC enterprise customers, 80% headcount growth in two years, larger Sydney HQ.

title: How Menulog Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Ceased operations 26 Nov 2025 after ~20 years, stuck at 5-10% share against a dominant Uber Eats.

title: How MilkRun Collapsed in the Australian Market
rationale: Ceased trading Apr 2023 (400+ redundancies); brand later sold to Woolworths at salvage value.

title: How Mimecast Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Successful channel-led ANZ entry (1,200+ customers); corporate outcome was acquisition by Permira for $5.8bn (2022).

title: How NCC Group Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Established government/critical-infrastructure ANZ presence from 2017 with ongoing policy engagement; steady, not high-growth.

title: How Nium Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Australia as revenue engine, deepening via NZ FSPR registration (2024) and expanding real-time payout capability.

title: How nPlan Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Early-stage entrant landing Australia's biggest road project (2025) alongside an Oct 2025 Series B; actively expanding.

title: How Onfido Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Pull-through ANZ entry via clients like Revolut; corporate outcome was acquisition by Entrust (2024).

title: How Peloton Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Still operating in Australia but persistently struggling (paused apparel, ACL issues); no clear failure/exit and no establishment — indeterminate.

title: How Pizza Hut Australia Faced a Franchisee Class Action
rationale: About a franchise-pricing class action (which the franchisor won) and franchisee insolvencies; Pizza Hut itself keeps operating — no company-level outcome.

title: How PropertyGuru Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Nuanced strategic-link case (REA 18% stake); PropertyGuru was taken private in 2024 as REA divested — end-state acquired.

title: How Quantexa Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: High-growth unicorn (108% FY2020 revenue growth, $1.8bn valuation) expanding across ANZ banks from Sydney/Melbourne.

title: How Revolut Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Actively expanding: from 3 staff/travel card to 1M Australian customers by 2026, layering products (crypto, stocks, lending).

title: How Sensat Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Recent (2023) government-backed Sydney entry described as becoming Australia's infrastructure visualisation platform; early and thin evidence.

title: How Sezzle Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Scaled back to a minimal Australian presence and ASX-delisted after the Zip merger collapsed — effective withdrawal from the market.

title: How Spectrum.Life Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Entered by acquiring three businesses in one day and planning 100+ new AU roles in 12 months; explicit expansion commitment.

title: How T-Pro Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Serial-acquisition expansion across ANZ (2022, 2023, 2025), PE-backed; actively building regional footprint.

title: How Thought Machine Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Established key challenger-bank clients (Judo, Kiwibank) with fast migrations; solid entry, $2.7bn valuation.

title: How Tines Entered the ANZ Market: From Security Automation to AI Workflows — Tines' APAC Beachhead Strategy
rationale: Unicorn (2025) doubling Australian headcount and launching an APAC hub (2026) on marquee customers (Canva, REA, ANU).

title: How Tractable Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Established with the dominant player — IAG (~70% of the market) as anchor — delivering quantified outcomes; unicorn.

title: How Uber Carshare Struggled in the Australian Market
rationale: Exited the Australian market in late 2025, leaving thousands of users without a platform.

title: How Valve / Steam Was Penalised by the ACCC in Australia
rationale: A$3M ACL penalty case; Steam continues to operate in Australia, so this is a regulatory penalty, not a market failure or establishment outcome.

title: How Viagogo Was Penalised by the ACCC in the Australian Market
rationale: A$7M ACL penalty for misleading pricing; Viagogo continues operating in Australia — regulatory penalty, not a company outcome.

title: How Volt Bank Collapsed in the Australian Market
rationale: Surrendered its banking licence and returned deposits in 2022 — the first Australian neobank to fail.

title: How Wayflyer Entered the ANZ Market: Funding Growth at the Speed of E-Commerce — Wayflyer's Australian Playbook
rationale: Treated Australia as a core growth market from 2021, deploying A$60M+ to 150+ brands; unicorn actively expanding.

title: How WeWork Collapsed in the Australian Market
rationale: Content explicitly rates the outcome 'Mixed' — the Australian network was durable and largely worked even as the global parent imploded; neither clean success nor failure.

title: How Wise Entered the ANZ Market
rationale: Established, large-scale ANZ position: 1M+ Australian customers, first fintech direct to the NPP, A$1bn+ balances held.
